title: How Model Context Protocol (MCP) actually works
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standardized protocol that enables AI models to discover and interact with external tools and data without custom integration code. MCP addresses the mismatch between deterministic APIs and probabilistic AI reasoning by providing a client-server architecture with core components including tools, prompts, resources, and context.
writeup: This video explains how the Model Context Protocol (MCP) lets AI models discover and call external tools and data sources without writing custom integration code for each one. It frames MCP as a way to bridge the gap between deterministic APIs and probabilistic AI reasoning, using a client-server architecture. The core components covered include tools, prompts, resources, and context. The standardization matters for building AI agents and multi-agent systems that connect to external systems in a consistent way.
